name: Pacific Fleet Submarine Museum
tagline: Walk through the USS Bowfin submarine and explore the museum that tells the story of the Silent Service — the perfect Pearl Harbor companion most visitors skip.
bestTime: Visit right after the USS Arizona memorial while you're already at Pearl Harbor. Morning crowds are lighter
parking: Free at Pearl Harbor Visitor Center, same lot
kamaaina: Kamaaina rates with valid Hawaii ID
talkStory: Formerly the USS Bowfin Submarine Museum, rebranded in 2023 as the Pacific Fleet Submarine Museum after a major renovation. The star is the USS Bowfin (SS-287), a WWII submarine launched in 1942 that completed nine war patrols — you can walk through the entire vessel and feel just how claustrophobic life was for 80 crew members. The museum itself covers the full history of the US submarine force, from the Civil War-era H.L. Hunley to modern nuclear subs. The outdoor Waterfront Memorial honors the 52 US submarines lost in WWII. Most Pearl Harbor visitors beeline for the Arizona Memorial and Missouri, but this is arguably the most hands-on, immersive experience in the entire Pearl Harbor complex. Kids love climbing through the sub.
